title: NBTC to embed OTT oversight in plan
desc: The National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission (NBTC) has reached a consensus that it has the authority to regulate over-the-top (OTT) platforms, and will embed this oversight in its upcoming Broadcasting and Television Master Plan (2026–2030).

title: Expert doubts viability of  govt policies
desc: A political science scholar is questioning the viability of the new government's policy platform, despite acknowledging its broad scope in addressing national challenges.

title: Julapun pledges aid for slain crew's kin
desc: Labour Minister Julapun Amornvivat expressed condolences to the families of three Thai crew members of the MV Mayuree Naree who were confirmed killed in an attack on the vessel in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: NACC delivers evidence  in MPs' lese-majeste case
desc: The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) delivered case files to the Supreme Court in a high-profile ethics case against 44 former Move Forward Party (MFP) MPs who signed a proposal to amend Section 112 of the Criminal Code, the lese majeste law.

title: Commission okays drone regulation reforms
desc: The National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission (NBTC) has approved a new set of draft regulations to expand drone spectrum use, upgrade technical standards, and strengthen oversight of drone operations nationwide.

title: Commission ramps up crackdown on investment scams
desc: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has intensified law enforcement actions to reinforce market credibility, as a sharp rise in investment scams, largely driven by social media, emerges as a growing systemic risk.

title: Premier ducks queries on legal action by refiners
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul declined to directly address concerns over private refiners preparing legal action against the government after it ordered a reduction in diesel refining margins, instead referring reporters to Energy Minister Akanat Promphan for clarification.

title: MPs grill govt on fuel plan
desc: The energy crisis and soaring fuel prices -- particularly the issue of refining margins -- dominated parliamentary debate during a joint sitting in parliament on Thursday, as opposition parties challenged the government's policy statement.

title: Merkle anticipates Bitcoin rebound in Q2
desc: Bitcoin prices are expected to rebound in the second quarter of 2026, supported by easing geopolitical tensions and a gradual return of risk appetite. However, prolonged conflicts that drive oil prices above US$100 per barrel could trigger a broad sell-off across risk assets, including cryptocurrencies, says Merkle Capital.

title: Pratunam flyover to close for 10 months for Orange Line construction
desc: The Pratunam flyover on Phetchaburi Road, Bangkok, will be closed to traffic for about 10 months from late April as tunnelling for the Orange Line enters a critical underground phase, the Mass Rapid Transit Authority of Thailand (MRTA) announced on Thursday.

title: Surging costs dull Songkran spirits
desc: Soaring fuel costs are rippling across Thailand just days before the Songkran festival, pushing up food prices, squeezing small vendors, raising costs for farmers and transport operators, and dampening travel sentiment in key tourism centres.

title: Merchant in hot water over 'fake gems'
desc: The Consumer Protection Police Division (CPPD) has arrested “Nicha”, a well-known online vendor of luxury goods, for allegedly deceiving customers by selling synthetic diamonds marketed as natural, in a case with reportedly more than 100 victims.

title: Fake coconut water alert after factories inspected
desc: The Ombudsman has called for an accelerated crackdown on the production of fake coconut water, following the arrest of operators at two processing facilities in Samut Songkhram province.

title: Thailand seizes B8.27bn more from regional scam network
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul on Thursday announced the seizure and freezing of a further 8.27 billion baht in assets linked to a transnational scam network, pushing the total value of confiscated assets in the case to more than 20 billion baht.

title: Songkran spending set to fall this year
desc: The University of the Thai Chamber of Commerce (UTCC) has revised down its forecast for this year’s Songkran spending to as low as 120 billion baht, from a previous 129.6 billion baht, citing rising diesel prices.

title: Tourism minister chasing B3-trillion goal
desc: Thailand’s tourism minister has vowed to restore tourism revenue to more than 3 trillion baht, exceeding pre-pandemic levels within the new government’s four-year term, and will proceed with a 300-baht tourism fee, as adopted in many countries.

title: Songkran 2026 around Bangkok: where to make merit then get soaked
desc: Songkran in 2026 is best tackled like Bangkok traffic: pick your lane early, commit, and keep a dry change of clothes within reach. The Thai New Year’s water fights still dominate the headlines, but this year’s smartest day trips mix temple-time calm with big-city spectacle, from Rattanakosin’s most photogenic blessings to Samut Prakan’s late-April Mon traditions —...

title: Policy statement lacks heart and clarity, says Abhisit
desc: Democrat Party leader Abhisit Vejjajiva on Thursday delivered a scathing critique of Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul’s policy statement, accusing the government of lacking clarity, substance and empathy for the public, while failing to address pressing security and economic challenges.

title: Central bank chief expects no change in interest rate
desc: The Bank of Thailand will keep its policy interest rate ​on hold, with ‌economic growth projected at 1.3% to 1.7% this year, governor Vitai Ratanakorn said on Thursday.

title: New movies out this week: Apr 9-15
desc: Five new releases that hit cinemas in Thailand this week.

title: David Bowie’s childhood home to open to public
desc: BROMLEY, England - With its grey front door and battered picket fence, No.4 Plaistow Grove in the anonymous London suburb of Bromley is unremarkable in every way except for one.

title: Huge bets on oil plunge placed just before ceasefire news
desc: LONDON - Investors placed approximately $950 million in bets on oil prices falling just hours before the United States and Iran announced a ceasefire, the latest large wager on the direction of the ​world’s most traded commodity ahead ⁠of a major policy announcement by President Donald Trump.

title: Miss Grand Thailand contestant secures global attention with hip hop dance
desc: Miss Grand Thailand contestant secures global attention with hip hop dance Legacy A contestant from Kalasin province gained international attention after a viral hip hop dance moves during the Miss Grand Thailand 2026 competition in Bangkok on March 28. The final of Miss Grand Thailand 2026 was held at MGI Hall Bravo BKK, with Pattama “Ning” Jitsawat from Chon Buri crowned the winner. However, Darathorn “Dada” Yoothong , representing Kalasin, drew widespread attention despite placing in the top 20. Dada went viral after performing a hip hop dance during the swimsuit segment, a round where cont

title: Two killed as Udon Thani wildfire crew pickup hits power pole
desc: Two killed as Udon Thani wildfire crew pickup hits power pole Legacy A pickup truck carrying park officials back from a wildfire operation crashed into a power pole on a sharp bend in Udon Thani yesterday evening, April 8, leaving two men dead and four others injured . The crash was reported at about 6pm on the Ban Kham Bon Wiang Chai road, Tan Lian subdistrict, Kut Chap district. Photo via สํานักบริหารพื้นที่อนุรักษ์ที่ 10 อุดรธานี The vehicle, a black single-cab pickup belonging to the Department of National Parks, Wildlife and Plant Conservation, left the road, hit a roadside power pole and

title: Thai stepfather kills toddler, tries to revive him with electric shock
desc: Thai stepfather kills toddler, tries to revive him with electric shock Legacy A Thai stepfather confessed to fatally abusing a one year old boy and shocking the child with an electric fishing device in an attempt to revive him. Officers from Khao Din Police Station in Ratchaburi questioned the child’s mother, 30 year old Saowarot, and stepfather, 38 year old Surasak, after the boy, identified as Khai, died under suspicious circumstances on April 6. A doctor told police that the boy died from injuries caused by a hard object that severely affected his brain. The child was also found with multip

title: Fake diamond seller arrested over 4 million baht losses
desc: Fake diamond seller arrested over 4 million baht losses Legacy The owner of a jewellery sales page with nearly 100,000 followers was arrested today, April 9, for allegedly selling fake diamonds and gems through livestreams while claiming they were genuine diamonds, with victims reporting losses of more than 4 million baht . The suspect, identified as 45 year old Nicha, was arrested in Bang Luang, Mueang Pathum Thani. She was wanted under two Criminal Court warrants issued on April 7. According to investigators, she used the page to sell fake gemstones in livestreams by presenting them as genui

title: Gold prices projected to hit $6,000 by third quarter
desc: Gold prices are expected to reach US$6,000 an ounce in the third quarter of this year, as bullion soared sharply after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Wednesday, even though short-term volatility is expected amid lingering uncertainties, traders say.

title: Butler breaks silence on Teresa Teng’s death in Chiang Mai 31 years on
desc: Butler breaks silence on Teresa Teng’s death in Chiang Mai 31 years on Legacy A Thai man, who said he was the butler of Taiwanese singer Teng Li-Chun , recalled the events before her death in Chiang Mai , claiming heavy traffic delayed treatment during a medical emergency . Teng, one of the most influential Chinese-speaking singers, died on May 8, 1995, while on holiday at a hotel in Chiang Mai province at the age of 42. A Thai doctor reported that she died from heart failure, while several news reports said she had suffered a severe asthma attack. Thirty-one years after her death, a Tha

title: 中国光模块巨头Terahop扩展在泰规模 本地采购超50%
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月9日，泰国投资促进委员会（BOI）秘书长纳立表示，他参加并庆祝了Terahop（泰国）有限公司在泰运营5周年活动，并参观了该公司位于沙拉武里府WHA工业园区的工厂。该公司为中国企业，是面向AI与数据中心产业的光纤通信设备与系统制造商。报道称，该工厂为沙拉武里府规模最大的工厂，目前公司正在阿瑜陀耶府建设第2座工厂。 据介绍，Terahop自2021年进入泰国投资并持续扩张，累计投资额超过300亿泰铢，雇用员工超过10000人，其中从事工程技术及研发（R&D）人员超过600人。本地原材料使用比例超过50%。BOI表示，泰国已成为Terahop的主要生产基地，覆盖研发、生产、组装与测试等环节，产品主要出口至美国与欧洲，客户为Google、AWS、Microsoft、Meta等大型数字企业，体现出国际企业对泰国作为高端技术产业投资基地的信心。 BOI称，Terahop在光连接解决方案领域处于领先地位，拥有全球第1的市场份额。公司在上一年度收入增长超过60%。其产品与技术可支持最高1600Gbps的高速光纤通信，并计划在近期实现进一步提升，速度提升幅度为当前的2至3倍，同时还可降低能源消耗。相关技术被认为是AI与数据中心等高算力场景所需的新一代数据互联关键技术。 Terahop拥有国际化网络。在美国与客户设有联合研发中心；在新加坡设有总部与业务管理中心

title: BTS to kick off world tour after landmark Seoul comeback
desc: SEOUL - South Korean K-pop megastars BTS will kick off their world tour on Thursday, riding the momentum of a chart-topping comeback album and a landmark performance in the heart of Seoul.

title: Iran-US ceasefire agreed but Hormuz, Lebanon disputes cloud the truce
desc: Iran-US ceasefire agreed but Hormuz, Lebanon disputes cloud the truce Legacy A conditional two-week Iran-US ceasefire took effect yesterday, April 8, halting more than 40 days of coordinated US-Israeli military strikes on Iran that had disrupted global energy markets and raised fears of a wider regional conflict. Pakistan brokered the truce, with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announcing the cessation of hostilities this past Wednesday in a post on X. The ceasefire, already described as “fragile”, came into effect roughly two hours before a deadline set by US President Donald Trump,

title: Mercedes driver doges petrol bill, station worker forced to cover costs
desc: Mercedes driver doges petrol bill, station worker forced to cover costs Legacy A woman driving a white Mercedes-Benz allegedly left a petrol station in Thung Yai, Hat Yai, Songkhla, with a 1,000 baht fuel bill unpaid on April 6, leaving the employee to cover the cost himself . The incident occurred at 2.42pm when a woman aged around 30 to 35 arrived in a white Mercedes-Benz and asked to fill benzine 95 worth 2,000 baht. She reportedly later reduced the amount to 1,000 baht as the pump was dispensing slowly. Photo via Khaosod An employee, 23 year old Theerapong, said the woman attempted to pay

title: Prospect sees e-commerce driving warehouse demand
desc: Industrial estate developer Prospect Development expects greater diversification among global suppliers relocating to Thailand, alongside continued growth in e-commerce, to drive demand for ready-built factories and warehouse space.

title: Kao launches Attack campaign showcasing ‘Fragrance Capsule Technology’ for long-lasting freshness
desc: Kao launches Attack campaign showcasing ‘Fragrance Capsule Technology’ for long-lasting freshness Legacy Kao Industrial (Thailand) Co., Ltd. , a Japanese manufacturer of consumer and chemical products, has launched a new campaign for Attack laundry detergent under the concept “To make clothes more fragrant, this moment calls for Attack: Pat, Pull, and Hug.” The company said the campaign highlights Fragrance Capsule Technology , designed to release fragrance throughout the day through physical movement, targeting modern consumers, including working families and younger people with f

title: Police detain 36 Chinese nationals in Pattaya nightclub raid
desc: Police detain 36 Chinese nationals in Pattaya nightclub raid Legacy Police in Pattaya raided a nightclub in Nong Prue, Bang Lamung, Chon Buri early today, April 9, arresting 36 Chinese nationals suspected of gathering for drug use . The operation took place at 3.30am after police received a tip-off about alleged drug activity inside a venue on Pattaya Third Road in North Pattaya. More than 40 officers surrounded and entered the premises. The building was identified as a four-storey luxury property, with each floor arranged into karaoke rooms . Officers found tourists occupying two rooms and mo

title: Israeli national arrested for nominee-run travel agency in Phuket
desc: Israeli national arrested for nominee-run travel agency in Phuket Legacy Police arrested an Israeli national in Phuket yesterday, April 8, for operating a travel agency illegally through a Thai nominee . Officers from the Department of Tourism and Tourist Police investigated illegal travel agencies in Phuket and found two companies in breach of the law. The first company, Gmat Hospitality, was found to be operating through a Thai nominee. Investigators said the company changed its board structure after receiving its operating licence and was later found to have two directors, one Thai national

title: Grab rolls out AI companion services to boost earnings
desc: Grab is accelerating its shift beyond transaction-based services to become an "intelligent everyday guide", evolving from a super-app into a more integrated, AI-driven companion.

title: 宋干节期间泰国各大机场推出停车福利迎出行高峰
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月8日，泰国机场集团（AOT）CEO帕维娜表示，宋干节期间民众返乡与旅游出行增加。AOT是泰国6大主要机场运营方，旗下包括素万那普机场、廊曼机场、清迈机场、清莱皇太后机场、普吉机场和合艾机场，其已对4月10日至19日的航空运输量作出预测，并与去年同期对比（数据截至2026年4月3日）如下： AOT预计，宋干节期间旅客吞吐量合计约370万人次，同比增长2.3%。其中国内旅客约144万人次，同比增长9.3%；国际旅客约226万人次，同比下降2.2%，AOT称主要受中东地区冲突影响。 AOT预计，航班量合计约23070架次，同比增长2.6%。其中国内航班约10918架次，同比增长4.5%；国际航班约12152架次，同比增长0.9%。 帕维娜还表示，为保障节日期间服务，AOT将在宋干节期间于素万那普机场、廊曼机场、普吉机场和合艾机场提供免费停车区域，安排如下： 1.素万那普机场长期停车区C区，自2026年4月11日00时01分至2026年4月15日24时00分提供24小时往返航站楼接驳车服务。 2.廊曼机场货运仓库4号楼区域，自2026年4月11日00时01分至2026年4月15日24时00分提供24小时往返航站楼接驳车服务。 3.普吉机场X-Terminal大楼周边停车区，自2026年4月10日18时00分至2026年4月15日23时59分可免费停车。 4.

title: 泰国新政府向国会提交施政纲要
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月9日，泰国总理兼内政部长阿努廷在国会大厦发表政府施政纲要，阐述内阁未来施政框架。此次会议为期两天，参众两院议员全部到场出席会议。 阿努廷表示，政府施政将遵循三项核心原则，即维护国家核心制度、坚持以国王为国家元首的民主制度以及以良政与法制为基础推进国家治理。在此框架下，政府规划五大政策领域，涵盖经济、外交与安全、社会事务、灾害与环境以及政府行政管理与法律改革，整体文件共19页。 本次政策声明及讨论共安排32小时30分钟。其中，总理宣读政策1小时30分钟，内阁成员发言6小时，参议院发言4小时，执政党众议员发言5小时30分钟，反对派众议员发言14小时30分钟，会议主持人预留1小时。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan） The post 泰国新政府向国会提交施政纲要 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: Mai Davika以新角色亮相《冰湖重生》发布会
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月8日，泰国人气演员Mai Davika飞赴海外，出席中国大型剧集《冰湖重生》发布会，在热烈的现场氛围中亮相，并与主演李昀锐同台互动，引发粉丝阵阵尖叫，在现场掀起热潮。 活动中，Mai Davika以耀眼造型登场，尽显超级明星气场，成为全场焦点。同时也公布了她在剧中的角色，燕北之主。她是一位极具权势与威严的人物，并配有一句气场十足的台词：“我会助你稳坐王位，再无人敢心生叛意。”更让观众对剧情充满期待。 《冰湖重生》作为经典作品《楚乔传》的续作，曾在亚洲范围内掀起现象级热潮。本季由李昀锐与黄杨钿甜担任主演，故事承接第一部结局展开，讲述角色宇文玥从冰湖中重生后的发展。 粉丝们可以提前锁定播出时间， 《冰湖重生》将于4月8日正式上线，并可通过TrueID观看泰语字幕版本。本剧话题度十足，值得期待！ （编译：hansa ；审核：wan；来源：sanook ） The post Mai Davika以新角色亮相《冰湖重生》发布会 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 芭提雅警方突袭KTV会所 36名中国人疑涉吸毒被捕
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月9日凌晨，芭堤雅市警察局局长率队，会同泰国东部二区侦查人员，动用40余名警力，对芭堤雅北部Pattaya Sai 3路一处俱乐部实施了包围搜查。警方称，此前接获线报称该处疑似有人在建筑内秘密聚众吸食毒品。 警方通报称，该建筑为一栋4层豪华建筑，每层被改造为卡拉OK包厢形式。行动时发现有游客正在2间包厢内活动。警方随即对其实施控制并展开了搜查。期间有人试图逃离，但警方最终将相关36人全部控制，并带回芭堤雅市警局进行进一步讯问。 警方表示，初步搜查未发现毒品或其他违法物品，推测相关人员可能在警方进入前已将疑似证物丢弃。相关人员均为中国公民，后续将对其进行体内毒品筛查并核查其入境文件，确认其是否依法入境并停留在泰国。 目前已将36人全部送检，并同步核查证件材料，之后将移交侦查人员按法律程序处理。 （编译：hansa ；审核：wan；来源：matichon ） The post 芭提雅警方突袭KTV会所 36名中国人疑涉吸毒被捕 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 王毅会见泰国公主诗琳通
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 2026年4月8日，中共中央政治局委员、外交部长王毅在北京会见泰国公主诗琳通。 王毅表示，2025年中泰建交50周年之际，哇集拉隆功国王陛下首次对华国事访问，习近平主席同他就推进中泰命运共同体建设达成重要共识，为深化中泰友好指明了方向。 王毅说，公主殿下45年来访华57次，足迹踏遍中国大江南北，是中国改革开放进程的亲历者、中国式现代化的见证人，也是促进中泰友好的实干家。你是中国人民的老朋友、好朋友，为深化中泰友好倾注了大量心血，作出了突出贡献，习近平主席专门为你颁发“友谊勋章”。今年是中国“十五五”开局之年，中方愿同泰方一道，以两国元首重要共识为根本遵循，弘扬中泰传统友好，在殿下关心的高科技、教育、民生等领域与泰方加强合作，不断播撒友好的种子，推动中泰命运共同体建设开花结果，造福人民。 诗琳通表示，中国是泰国的好朋友。泰方赞赏中方在人才培养、人力资源等领域给予泰方的大力支持。我此次访华参观了感兴趣的高科技项目，收获很大。希望同中方进一步密切各领域沟通交流，加强科技、农业、医学、文化、教育等方面务实合作，促进两国人民相知相亲，推进泰中友好事业枝繁叶茂。 （来源：中国驻泰国大使馆） The post 王毅会见泰国公主诗琳通 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .
